message:
fix(process): preserve Command stdio configuration across output/status calls

Command::output() and status() previously mutated self's stdin/stdout/stderr
fields to set up capture (Pipe) or passthrough (Inherit), but never restored
the original configuration. This meant a subsequent spawn() on the same
Command instance would use the output/status overrides instead of whatever
the caller had configured, silently changing behavior.

The fix introduces spawn_with_temporary_stdio() which saves the current
stdio fields, replaces them for the spawn, and restores the originals
afterward (even on spawn failure). Both sync and async variants of
output() and status() now use this helper.

Tests added:
- test_command_output_preserves_stdio_configuration
- test_command_output_async_preserves_stdio_configuration
- test_command_status_preserves_stdio_configuration
- test_command_status_async_preserves_stdio_configuration

Each test configures a specific stdio mode, calls output/status, then
verifies a subsequent spawn() still uses the original configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
